channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Some Republicans expected to oppose $1B request for Secret Service and ballroom
item.description: As Republicans work to approve a reconciliation bill to fund ICE, some in the party are pushing back against a proposal to include $1 billion in funding for Secret Service and Trump's ballroom.
item.content:encoded: As Republicans work to approve a reconciliation bill to fund ICE, some in the party are pushing back against a proposal to include $1 billion in funding for Secret Service and Trump's ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Chuck Todd on what the tensions between Trump and his party means for his agenda
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks political analyst and podcast host Chuck Todd what the tension between the president and Republicans in Congress means for Trump's agenda.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks political analyst and podcast host Chuck Todd what the tension between the president and Republicans in Congress means for Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Colorado Democratic Party censures Gov. Jared Polis over commutation for Tina Peters
item.description: Colorado's Democratic Party voted to formally censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting the sentence of Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted over a plot to promote false 2020 election claims.
item.content:encoded: Colorado's Democratic Party voted to formally censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting the sentence of Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted over a plot to promote false 2020 election claims.
item.dc:creator: Bente Birkeland

item.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
item.description: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.
item.content:encoded:
About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Republicans work to pass reconciliation bill but ballroom money may get left out, Trump says Gulf allies persuaded him not to resume attacks on Iran, U.S. indicts Cuba's former President Raúl Castro.
item.content:encoded: Republicans work to pass reconciliation bill but ballroom money may get left out, Trump says Gulf allies persuaded him not to resume attacks on Iran, U.S. indicts Cuba's former President Raúl Castro.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Public libraries struggle to get new books on shelves after distributor shuts down
item.description: Libraries are struggling to source materials following the closure of the nation's largest book distributor earlier this year.
item.content:encoded: Libraries are struggling to source materials following the closure of the nation's largest book distributor earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: U.S. indicts former Cuban President Raúl Castro
item.description: The U.S. indicted Cuba's former President Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two civilian planes -- a dramatic move that could further inflame tensions between Washington and Havana.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. indicted Cuba's former President Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two civilian planes -- a dramatic move that could further inflame tensions between Washington and Havana.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Iran war disrupt Mecca pilgrimage plans for some American Muslims
item.description: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.content:encoded: Making the pilgrimage to Mecca is a once-in-a-lifetime experience and a sacred obligation for many Muslims. But the ongoing Iran war is making some American Muslims reconsider their plans.
item.dc:creator: Nargis Rahman

item.title: Miami shelter for immigrant children closing doors after federal funding cut
item.description: A 60-year-old shelter for immigrant children run by Catholic Charities in Miami is shutting down due to curbed federal funds.
item.content:encoded: A 60-year-old shelter for immigrant children run by Catholic Charities in Miami is shutting down due to curbed federal funds.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

item.title: South African court hears case over whether last elephants in zoo should be removed
item.description: As South Africa's last zoo elephants become the focus of a landmark court battle, activists say the animals' quiet suffering is impossible to ignore.
item.content:encoded: As South Africa's last zoo elephants become the focus of a landmark court battle, activists say the animals' quiet suffering is impossible to ignore.
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett
